essence: Tests, inspects, and maintains fire dampers to prevent fire and smoke spreading through ventilation systems.

identity.title: Fire Damper Technician
identity.summary: Inspects, tests, and repairs fire dampers within HVAC systems, ensuring they close correctly and comply with fire‑safety regulations.

salary.notes: Technicians specialising in large commercial HVAC networks or complex industrial systems can exceed £45k-£55k+.

qualifications: Training in fire‑damper inspection and maintenance
Knowledge of HVAC systems and fire‑safety regulations
Ability to test damper operation and identify faults
Understanding of ductwork, access requirements, and compliance standards

lifestyle.description: Fire damper technicians work across commercial buildings, hospitals, schools, and industrial sites inspecting and testing fire dampers. The role involves accessing ductwork, verifying damper closure, documenting compliance, and ensuring ventilation systems meet fire‑safety standards. It suits individuals who enjoy practical technical work, problem‑solving, and contributing to building safety.

habits: Follows strict inspection and reporting standards
Keeps accurate compliance and maintenance records
Communicates clearly with building managers and contractors
Identifies faults in ductwork and damper mechanisms
